source_key: 14.1 The mapping itself is an object
title: The mapping itself is an object
role: Make the shift from action to object explicit and almost physical.
prompt_core: Illustrate a clear but elegant machine that takes in one kind of input and reliably produces one output, while the machine itself is highlighted as the real object of attention. Avoid childish gears; think transparent scientific instrument or sculptural mechanism. The reader should feel that a rule can itself be studied, combined, inverted, and transformed.
caption: A function is not just what it does; it is also a thing in its own right.
master_preamble: Create a visually sophisticated illustration for an adult mathematics book written for neurodivergent visual thinkers learning from first principles. The image must clarify the concept rather than merely decorate it. Favor spatial legibility, tactile materials, intellectually serious editorial art direction, and one dominant idea per frame. Keep geometry believable, lighting restrained, and enough quiet negative space for a caption or later overlay.
negative_block: No classroom clip art, no chalkboard wallpaper, no random floating equations, no childish mascots, no busy collage, no gibberish text, no impossible geometry, no decorative sci-fi fog, no glossy stock-photo students. If exact labels, axes, or theorem marks are needed, leave clean space for them to be overlaid later.
api_prompt:
Create a visually sophisticated illustration for an adult mathematics book written for neurodivergent visual thinkers learning from first principles. The image must clarify the concept rather than merely decorate it. Favor spatial legibility, tactile materials, intellectually serious editorial art direction, and one dominant idea per frame. Keep geometry believable, lighting restrained, and enough quiet negative space for a caption or later overlay.

Project context:
This image belongs to a rigorous adult mathematics book for neurodivergent visual thinkers.
It should function as a cognitive bridge for subsection "14.1 The mapping itself is an object".
Treat it as editorial art with pedagogical duty.

Image role:
Make the shift from action to object explicit and almost physical.

Layout intent:
H — Half-page conceptual plate

Style direction:
function-machine plate

Core image brief:
Illustrate a clear but elegant machine that takes in one kind of input and reliably produces one output, while the machine itself is highlighted as the real object of attention. Avoid childish gears; think transparent scientific instrument or sculptural mechanism. The reader should feel that a rule can itself be studied, combined, inverted, and transformed.

Caption intent for layout reference only:
A function is not just what it does; it is also a thing in its own right.

Overlay / production note:
No mandatory overlay note.

Rendering instructions:
Do not place explanatory typography, equations, or labels inside the art unless they are intrinsic physical objects in the scene.
Preserve clear compositional space for later book-layout overlays where appropriate.
Make the concept immediately graspable to an intelligent adult who distrusts rote schooling and learns through spatial pattern, tactile intuition, and elegant structure.
Keep the mood intellectually serious, lucid, and memorable rather than cute.

Negative constraints:
No classroom clip art, no chalkboard wallpaper, no random floating equations, no childish mascots, no busy collage, no gibberish text, no impossible geometry, no decorative sci-fi fog, no glossy stock-photo students. If exact labels, axes, or theorem marks are needed, leave clean space for them to be overlaid later.

source_key: 14.2 The graph makes the function visible
title: The graph makes the function visible
layout_label: Hybrid diagram plate; overlay exact geometry/labels later if needed
role: Turn mapping into a visible curve in the plane.
prompt_core: Show a set of input-output pairs rising into the plane and cohering into a continuous graph, with the curve felt as the visible body of the function. The image should treat the graph not as homework paper but as the function made visible. Elegant coordinate aesthetics, transparent grid, optional overlay for axes and points.
caption: A graph is the function showing its body.
overlay: Overlay axes and sample points later.

source_key: 14.3 Composition is feeding one machine into another
title: Composition is feeding one machine into another
role: Embodied composition as output flowing into the next input.
prompt_core: Create a transparent two-stage machine or pipe system where the output of the first transformation literally feeds the input of the second, producing a final transformed result. The scene should make function composition feel practical and spatial. Clean, glass-like, adult, with a sense of process rather than clutter.
caption: Composition means do one transformation, then feed its result into the next.
master_preamble: Create a visually sophisticated illustration for an adult mathematics book written for neurodivergent visual thinkers learning from first principles. The image must clarify the concept rather than merely decorate it. Favor spatial legibility, tactile materials, intellectually serious editorial art direction, and one dominant idea per frame. Keep geometry believable, lighting restrained, and enough quiet negative space for a caption or later overlay.
negative_block: No classroom clip art, no chalkboard wallpaper, no random floating equations, no childish mascots, no busy collage, no gibberish text, no impossible geometry, no decorative sci-fi fog, no glossy stock-photo students. title: Inverse functions and reflection
layout_label: Hybrid diagram plate; overlay exact geometry/labels later if needed
role: Show inversion as input-output swapping and reflection across y=x.
prompt_core: Illustrate a graph and its inverse as reflections across a luminous diagonal mirror line representing y equals x. The swap of input and output should be visible both as mirrored curve geometry and as a conceptual exchange of roles. Elegant and precise, with strong visual symmetry and room for optional annotation overlays.
caption: Inverse functions are the same relation viewed with input and output exchanged.
overlay: Overlay diagonal line and axes after generation.

source_key: 14.5 Historical note: Euler and the rise of function-thinking
title: Historical note: Euler and the rise of function-thinking
role: Place the abstraction of function in its historical emergence.
prompt_core: Create a historical editorial scene where curves, formulas, and mappings begin to be treated as coherent objects in their own right. You can suggest Euler-era notation and paper culture without turning it into a portrait piece. The conceptual birth of function-thinking should dominate, not biographical trivia.
caption: Functions became powerful when the mapping itself became an object of thought.

title: Thought experiments
role: Invite learners to think in mappings, graphs, and inverses.
prompt_core: Show a tiny input-output machine, its graph in the plane, and a reflected inverse partner, all in one sparse triad. The point is to let the learner ask: what stays the same when I switch viewpoints? Minimal, clear, encouraging.
caption: Move between machine view and graph view until they feel like the same thing.
master_preamble: Create a visually sophisticated illustration for an adult mathematics book written for neurodivergent visual thinkers learning from first principles. The image must clarify the concept rather than merely decorate it. Favor spatial legibility, tactile materials, intellectually serious editorial art direction, and one dominant idea per frame.
